assertions.title: QUALITY_METHODOLOGY.md exists and covers categories/scoring/code execution

steps.action: Run Python content checks on QUALITY_METHODOLOGY.md
steps.expected: File exists and includes 6 categories, weighted formula, and code execution+sandbox approach
steps.observed: File exists; categories found: coding/reasoning/math/instruction following/writing/tool calling; weighted formula present; code execution+sandbox terms present

assertions.title: Scoring formula and tier weights in doc match implementation

steps.expected: Easy=1x, Hard=2x, Expert=3x, Tool Calling=3x and matching weighted-score formula
steps.observed: Doc weight rows matched 1x/2x/3x/3x and implementation TIER_WEIGHTS={'easy':1,'hard':2,'expert':3,'tool_calling':3}; formula text matched

assertions.title: Documented problem counts match code lists (15/10/16/40=81)

steps.action: Import problem lists and count per tier
steps.expected: 15 easy, 10 hard, 16 expert, 40 tool calling, total 81
steps.observed: Counts from code: easy=15, hard=10, expert=16, tool_calling=40, total=81

steps.action: Run targeted module-structure test for list counts

assertions.title: README Quality section links to QUALITY_METHODOLOGY.md

steps.action: Check README Quality Benchmarks section for methodology link
steps.expected: A markdown link to QUALITY_METHODOLOGY.md exists in quality section
steps.observed: README contains 'For detailed methodology, see [Quality Benchmark Methodology](QUALITY_METHODOLOGY.md)' under Quality Benchmarks

assertions.title: Contamination resistance docs include variants and ProblemSource extensibility

steps.expected: Docs mention parameterized variants and ProblemSource pluggable interface/extensibility
steps.observed: Contamination Resistance section present; Parameterized Variant System documented; ProblemSource abstract pluggable interface documented

steps.action: Run targeted pytest docstring checks for ProblemSource
